# /tdd brief ŌĆö kw_user_generated_sentinel_markers (task 13.7b ŌĆö adopt osb's `@user`/`@generated` sentinel-marker vocabulary for KnowledgeWriter human-section preservation: an EXPLICIT `@user` human-owned region + `@generated` writer-region recognition + the mandatory neutralizer extension; ADDITIVE STRENGTHENING ONLY, dormant, sole-writer safety-critical)

## Feature
Adopt osb's `@user` / `@generated` sentinel-marker vocabulary onto the KnowledgeWriter's EXISTING human-section-preservation machinery ŌĆö **purely additively**. Today human ownership is IMPLICIT (the unmarked complement of a `<!-- kw:region:<id> -->` assistant region; `enforceHumanOwnership` protects it). This slice adds an **EXPLICIT `@user` human-owned region** (so a human region sitting BETWEEN two assistant regions can be individually delimited + protected ŌĆö which the unmarked-complement model cannot do) + recognition of `@generated` as a writer-owned region (osb-vault interop; == `kw:region` semantics), + the **mandatory neutralizer extension** so a content-embedded `@user`/`@generated` sentinel can never forge a region boundary (the Lesson-9 invariant, extended to the new vocabulary). Ships **DORMANT** (no real osb-vault I/O; exercised by fixtures/tests). It is the confinement primitive the deferred 13.8 (living-vault autonomous synthesis) rests on ("a `@user` region is provably never overwritten").

> ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ **OWNER-AUTHORIZED SOLE-WRITER CHANGE ŌĆö ADDITIVE STRENGTHENING ONLY.** This touches `enforceHumanOwnership` (the sole autonomous Markdown writer's ownership gate ŌĆö safety rule 1, KN-4/KN-9, forbidden-pattern #4). It MUST NEVER weaken the sole-writer / one-writer / human-section-preservation defaults: the unmarked-complement-is-human default STAYS, every existing ownership/sections test STILL PASSES (regression pins), and human protection only ever GROWS (a `@user` region becomes ADDITIONALLY protected; nothing formerly-protected becomes writable). **If the architecturally-correct design would weaken ANY sole-writer / human-ownership guarantee, STOP and escalate to `orchestrator-2` (ŌåÆ lead) ŌĆö that is beyond the authorization.** MANDATORY Step-8 adversarial review (security + code-quality).

## Use case + traceability
- **Task ID:** 13.7 (the marker-vocabulary bullet; the block-provenance bullet is the SEPARATE follow-up slice (b)). `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` 13.7: "osb's `@generated`/`@user` sentinel-marker vocabulary is ADOPTED for KnowledgeWriter's marker-bounded human-section preservation." Cross-doc invariant: none.
- **Architecture sections it implements:** `ARCHITECTURE.md ┬¦6` (KnowledgeWriter sole writer + human-section preservation ŌĆö KN-4/KN-9/KN-8) + `┬¦13` (Obsidian Second Brain inheritance ŌĆö Obsidian-compatible marker vocabulary). Safety rule **1 (one writer / human-ownership preservation)** is the load-bearing invariant. Confirm ┬¦6/┬¦13 at Step 0.
- **The EXISTING machinery this layers onto (from recon ŌĆö read these at Step 0):**
  - `packages/knowledge/src/markdown-vault/sections.ts` ŌĆö `MARKER_RE = /<!-- (\/?)kw:region:([^\s>]+) -->/gu` (L38), `regionOpenMarker`/`regionCloseMarker`/`renderRegion` (L19-33), `parseSections` (L86) ŌåÆ ordered `HumanSection | AssistantSection[]`, `humanOwnedText` (L180), `upsertRegionBody` (L193). Human = unmarked complement (`HumanSection.kind === "human"`); assistant = `kw:region`-marked. NO human marker today.
  - `packages/knowledge/src/knowledge-writer/ownership.ts` ŌĆö `checkOwnership`/`enforceHumanOwnership` (L62/L126), the DEFAULT `ownershipCheck` in `writer.ts` `applyPlan` step 5 (L188, L225-236). Rejects (`ownership_violation`, typed, never thrown): `humanSignature(prior) !== humanSignature(next)` ŌćÆ `human_content_modified` (L118-120 ŌĆö the exact human-preservation guarantee); humanŌåÆregion absorption (L115-117); untargeted assistant region byte-instability (KN-8, L97-105); untargeted new region (L109-113).
  - `packages/workflows/src/activities/projections/noteSlug.ts` ŌĆö `neutralizeRegionMarkers` (L38, fixpoint `<!--`ŌåÆ`<\!--` escape, terminating + idempotent, POST-CONDITION: no `REGION_MARKER_RE`-matchable substring survives ŌĆö Lesson 9) + `REGION_MARKER_RE` (L23) + `neutralizeFrontmatterValue` (L60). Defuses ONLY `kw:region` today.
- **Prior art / test pattern:** `packages/knowledge/test/{sections,ownership}.test.ts`; briefs `006`/`007` (region-marker-neutralization + frontmatter-marker-safety) ŌĆö RED-first with a **clean-content no-op regression pin** + a **boundary-integrity round-trip** as the load-bearing safety cases; `packages/workflows/test/region-marker-neutralization.test.ts` (Lesson-9 fixpoint tests). LESSONS 9 + 10.

## Scope boundary (IN vs deferred)
- **IN (this slice ŌĆö ONE atomic safety unit; recognition + gate + neutralizer are coupled by the forge-boundary invariant and MUST ship together):**
  - `sections.ts`: recognize an EXPLICIT `@user` human-owned region + `@generated` writer-owned region in `parseSections`, ADDITIVE to the existing `kw:region` grammar. A `@user` region parses to a `HumanSection` (human-owned); a `@generated` region parses to an `AssistantSection` (writer-owned, == `kw:region`).
  - `ownership.ts`: `enforceHumanOwnership` additionally treats a `@user` region as human-owned + preserved (its `humanSignature` contribution is protected verbatim) ŌĆö ADDITIVE to the unmarked-complement default (which STAYS).
  - `noteSlug.ts`: extend `neutralizeRegionMarkers` / `REGION_MARKER_RE` to defuse content-embedded `@user`/`@generated` sentinels ŌĆö MANDATORY (recognition without this = a forge vector = a weakening). The Lesson-9 fixpoint/idempotence/post-condition invariant holds for the FULL vocabulary.
  - A **single-source-of-truth marker vocabulary** (or a parity test) so the parser and the neutralizer provably cover the SAME marker set (they cannot drift ŌĆö a marker the parser recognizes but the neutralizer misses is a forge vector).
- **DEFERRED (named ŌĆö do NOT build):** (1) task-(b) 13.7a numbered `(src:Bn)` block-provenance contract round (the owner's NEXT slice ŌĆö a frozen-contract change; separate). (2) real osb-vault read/write I/O (dormant injection point). (3) 13.8 living-vault synthesis that CONSUMES the `@user` confinement (deferred, real synthesis). (4) migrating existing `kw:region` notes to the `@user`/`@generated` vocabulary (not needed ŌĆö additive coexistence).

## Acceptance criteria (what "done" means ŌĆö every one is ADDITIVE; none weakens)
- [ ] **NEW: a `@user` region is preserved verbatim (additive protection):** a KnowledgeWriter commit whose plan would modify text inside a `@user` region is REJECTED (`ownership_violation` / `human_content_modified`, typed, never thrown) ŌĆö the same guarantee the unmarked complement already gets, now available EXPLICITLY for a human region between assistant regions.
- [ ] **REGRESSION PIN ŌĆö the unmarked-complement-is-human default is UNCHANGED:** every existing `ownership.test.ts` / `sections.test.ts` case still passes byte-for-byte; unmarked human text is still protected; no formerly-protected text becomes writable. (The review's core check.)
- [ ] **`@generated` recognized as writer-owned (interop, additive):** a `@generated` region parses as an `AssistantSection` (writer-owned / refreshable, == `kw:region`); recognizing it must NOT reclassify any currently-human (unmarked) text as writer-owned (that would weaken protection).
- [ ] **MANDATORY neutralizer parity (Lesson 9 extended):** a content-embedded `@user`/`@generated` sentinel is neutralized before it can forge a region boundary; `neutralizeRegionMarkers` stays terminating + idempotent (clean input byte-identical) with the POST-CONDITION that no parser-recognizable marker substring (kw:region OR `@user`/`@generated`) survives. A **parity test** proves the neutralizer defuses EVERY marker form the parser recognizes.
- [ ] **Clean-content no-op regression pin:** marker-free content is byte-identical through parse + neutralize (no false marker detection on ordinary prose containing `@user`/`@generated` as plain words ŌĆö mind word-boundary/exact-token matching, cf. Lesson 12's prose-false-positive trap).
- [ ] **No weakening (the guardrail):** no change reduces any sole-writer / one-writer / human-ownership guarantee; `enforceHumanOwnership` stays the default gate; the assistant-region byte-stability (KN-8) + no-untargeted-new-region + humanŌåÆregion-absorption rejections all still hold. If the correct design would weaken any of these ŌĆö STOP + escalate.
- [ ] `packages/knowledge` + `packages/workflows` `turbo typecheck test` green; repo-wide `pnpm -w turbo run typecheck test` green.

## RED test outline (extend `packages/knowledge/test/{sections,ownership}.test.ts` + `packages/workflows/test/region-marker-neutralization.test.ts`)
1. `user_region_parses_as_human` ŌĆö `parseSections` on a note with a `@user` region ŌćÆ that span is a `HumanSection`. Why: ┬¦6 explicit human ownership.
2. `generated_region_parses_as_assistant` ŌĆö a `@generated` region ŌćÆ `AssistantSection` (== `kw:region`). Why: osb interop.
3. `user_region_modification_rejected` ŌĆö a plan modifying `@user`-region text ŌćÆ `ownership_violation`/`human_content_modified` (typed). Why: additive human protection (safety rule 1).
4. `unmarked_human_still_protected_REGRESSION` ŌĆö the existing unmarked-complement protection is unchanged (re-run/extend the current human-content-modified + absorption cases). Why: NO WEAKENING.
5. `generated_recognition_does_not_reclassify_human` ŌĆö recognizing `@generated` does NOT turn any unmarked/human text into a writer-owned region. Why: NO WEAKENING.
6. `neutralizer_defuses_user_and_generated` ŌĆö content-embedded `@user`/`@generated` sentinels are neutralized (no forged boundary); fixpoint terminates + idempotent; post-condition no marker substring survives. Why: Lesson 9 extended.
7. `parser_neutralizer_parity` ŌĆö for EVERY marker form the parser recognizes, the neutralizer defuses it (single-source-of-truth / parity ŌĆö they can't drift). Why: a recognized-but-undefused marker is a forge vector.
8. `clean_content_noop_REGRESSION` ŌĆö marker-free prose (incl. the words "@user"/"@generated" in ordinary text, if the syntax is a bare token) is byte-identical through parse+neutralize (no false detection). Why: Lesson-12 prose-false-positive discipline.

## Cross-doc invariant impact
- **NONE** (plan 13.7: "Cross-doc invariant: none"). Additive marker recognition + gate + neutralizer, internal to `packages/knowledge` + `packages/workflows`. No frozen Appendix-A model / schema / snapshot changed. If the design surfaces a need to touch a frozen contract (e.g. a shared marker-vocabulary type in `@sow/contracts`), flag at Step 2.5 ŌĆö a frozen-contract move is a separate atomic doc-paired change, not silent.

## Things to flag at Step 2.5 (design questions ŌĆö default votes)
1. **`@user`/`@generated` marker SYNTAX.** Default vote: **HTML-comment form mirroring `kw:region`** ŌĆö `<!-- @user -->ŌĆ”<!-- /@user -->` / `<!-- @generated:<id> -->ŌĆ”` (Obsidian-INVISIBLE, neutralization-safe, consistent with the shipped grammar). Alternative: osb's native (possibly visible) `@generated`/`@user` tokens ŌĆö flag if the interop requirement demands the exact osb syntax (then the neutralizer + the false-positive discipline (Q's clean-content pin) get harder). Rationale: reuse the proven HTML-comment neutralization + parse path; least new attack surface. CONFIRM ŌĆö this is load-bearing (it sets the neutralizer regex + the false-positive boundary).
2. **Single-source-of-truth vocabulary vs parity test.** Default vote: **a shared marker-vocabulary definition consumed by BOTH `parseSections` and `neutralizeRegionMarkers`** (they cannot drift). If a shared constant would force a cross-package (`@sow/contracts`/`@sow/domain`) placement that feels heavy, fall back to a **parity test** asserting the neutralizer defuses every parser-recognized form. Either way the parity property MUST be pinned (criterion 4/7). Confirm which.
3. **`@user` composition with the unmarked-complement default.** Default vote: **human-owned = (unmarked complement) UNION (`@user` regions)** ŌĆö STRICTLY MORE protected, never less. A `@user` region is an ADDITIONAL explicit human span; unmarked text stays human. Confirm this is the semantics (and that `humanSignature` includes `@user`-region content so its modification is caught).
4. **`@generated` vs existing `kw:region` ŌĆö coexist or unify.** Default vote: **coexist** (recognize `@generated` as an alias-class of writer-owned region; do NOT migrate/rewrite existing `kw:region` notes ŌĆö additive). Confirm (a unify/migrate would be a larger, riskier change ŌĆö out of scope).
5. **Nesting / malformed markers.** Default vote: reuse the existing `parseSections` malformed-marker rejection posture (a `@user` open without close, or nested/overlapping `@user`/`kw:region`, is rejected/typed, not silently mis-parsed) ŌĆö mirror the current `sections.test.ts` malformed cases. Confirm the exact rejection semantics for a `@user` overlapping a `kw:region`.

## Wiring / entry point (Step 7.5)
- **Entry point:** `parseSections` + `enforceHumanOwnership` are already on the LIVE KnowledgeWriter commit path (`writer.ts` `applyPlan` step 5); `neutralizeRegionMarkers` is on the live projection path (`noteSlug.ts`). This slice EXTENDS those live gates ŌĆö so it is reachable on the real writer path (not a dormant-only addition); the DORMANT aspect is only that no real osb-vault supplies `@user`/`@generated` content yet (fixtures do). State this at Step 7.5.
- **Depends on:** the shipped region-marker arc (`sections.ts`/`ownership.ts`/`noteSlug.ts`, Lessons 9/10). **Blocks:** the deferred 13.8 living-vault synthesis (its `@user`-confinement rests on this).

## Estimated commit count
**1** (one atomic safety unit ŌĆö parser recognition + ownership gate + neutralizer must ship together; splitting leaves an unsafe intermediate where recognition exists without neutralization = a forge vector). This IS the safety-critical slice ŌåÆ its own focused commit, no unrelated work bundled. **Step-8 adversarial review MANDATORY** (fresh general-purpose Agent, security + code-quality): the change is ADDITIVE-ONLY (every existing sole-writer/ownership guarantee intact ŌĆö diff-verify the defaults; every existing test still green); a `@user` region is genuinely protected; the neutralizer defuses the FULL recognized vocabulary (parserŌåöneutralizer parity ŌĆö no forge vector); no prose false-positive; malformed/nested markers rejected. If the review finds ANY weakening of a sole-writer/human-ownership guarantee ŌåÆ STOP + escalate (owner authorization is additive-strengthening ONLY).

## Lessons-logged candidates (implementer flags Step 9)
- **Convention candidate** (implementer's call): "adopting a NEW marker vocabulary into the KnowledgeWriter is ADDITIVE-ONLY over the human-ownership default (human = unmarked-complement Ōł¬ explicit-`@user`, never less); recognition + gate + neutralizer ship as ONE atomic unit with a parserŌåöneutralizer PARITY invariant (a recognized-but-undefused marker is a forge vector ŌĆö Lesson 9), and clean-content stays a no-op (Lesson-12 prose-false-positive discipline)."
- **Architecture-doc note candidate:** the KnowledgeWriter now supports an EXPLICIT `@user` human-owned region (in addition to the implicit unmarked complement) ŌĆö the confinement primitive 13.8 depends on.

Step 0 ŌĆö read `sections.ts` (`MARKER_RE`/`parseSections`/`renderRegion`), `ownership.ts` (`checkOwnership`/`enforceHumanOwnership`/`humanSignature`), `noteSlug.ts` (`neutralizeRegionMarkers`/`REGION_MARKER_RE`), + the existing `sections.test.ts`/`ownership.test.ts`/`region-marker-neutralization.test.ts`. 2. Step 2.5 ŌĆö Q1 (syntax) + Q2 (parity mechanism) + Q3 (`@user` Ōł¬ default) are load-bearing ŌĆö ping me your write-up + design BEFORE writing cases. **If your architecturally-correct design would weaken any sole-writer / human-ownership guarantee, STOP and flag it to me ŌĆö do not proceed (owner authorization is additive-only).**
3. RED first ŌĆö regression pins (existing protection intact) + the new `@user` protection + parserŌåöneutralizer parity + clean-content no-op are the load-bearing cases.
